text: Basis-Kataster Application: <insert application name here>		Examples	Who should work on the point?	 Provider of the service/host, as well as the registered office of any parent company		"The address of the provider is required here. e.g. HARTING IT Services Marienwerderstraße 2 32339 Espelkamp Germany"	HARTING GBU	 "Description (purpose or use case for the application) Current procedure: Desired procedure:"		"Current procedure: The current procedure for signing documents is as follows: The document is printed out, signed by hand, scanned and then sent by e-mail. Desired procedure: We receive the documents digitally and then sign them directly digitally using the requested service."	HARTING GBU	 What information is processed with the application?		"Research and development data (e.g. requirements and functional specifications), IT infrastructure (e.g. authorisation concept, network architecture), reports from internal IT systems, HR analyses, in-house expertise Manufacturing processes, design drawings, customer and supplier master data, purchase price information, Personnel data, employment contracts, order data, data for the preparation of strategic or personnel policy decisions, strategic business plans, compliance incidents, general work instructions, process and procedure descriptions, information and advertising brochures, product catalogue data, ..."	HARTING GBU	 Classification of information with regard to confidentiality ( unrestricted | restricted | confidential | strictly confidential):		"For a description of the classes, see the ""Classification"" tab."	Information owner (division, department management or process owner)	 "Detailed layout plan (architecture plan): - Which applications does the software communicate with? - Which protocols are used? - What are the communication directions?"		"Which applications does the software communicate with? The software is a standalone solution, but offers a few optional interfaces: - Single sign-on connection via SAML2.0 - LDAPS to synchronise custom attributes of AD users Which protocols are used? RDP, SSH, HTTPS, etc. What are the communication directions? HTTPS --> inbound RDP --> inbound"	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Initial set-up and subsequent expansion stages (3 years' consideration)		The use of the software is currently limited to the automatic recognition of invoice content. If we are satisfied with the data quality after one year, we want a connection to SAP so that the data can be entered automatically.	HARTING GBU	 Use at a single location or internationally?		Espelkamp only	HARTING GBU	 Has a system description been created?		Yes, the system description has been completed and accepted by IT.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Is there a separate user administration and who carries it out?		"Yes, user administration is carried out by Team xxx. This means that it is regularly checked whether all employees who are created in the service are actually still working for HARTING. are actually still working for HARTING."	HARTING GBU	 Should or how should the data be backed up and, if necessary, restored?		A data backup is not necessary	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Are there possibly even archiving obligations?		There are no archiving obligations	HARTING GBU	 Should local add-ins be installed and who operates/maintains them?		No local add-ins are required for the tool	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Is there an operating concept?		"The operating concept includes the following points: - How is the application distributed? - Who is authorised to use the application? - Who is responsible for user administration? - Who is responsible for carrying out updates? - Who takes care of support in the event of problems?"	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Number of users		10,All HARTING employees, department xxx, etc.	HARTING GBU	 How is the expected volume of data estimated?		High, Medium, Low	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Responsible division (company, department)		HARTING IT Services, Network & Security	HARTING GBU	 "Who provides support and answers any handling questions that may arise? (Who is the application owner?)"		Max Mustermann (Must be a HARTING employee)	HARTING GBU	 IT internal contacts / administrators		Hans Mustermann	HARTING GBU in consultation with IT, if one is required.	 Business Critical Assessment regarding the availability of the system or information (how critical is it if the application fails):		"Very High = Assets / information can lead to catastrophic business-critical losses or damage in the event of even a very brief failure or loss. High = Assets / information can lead to significant business-critical losses or damage in the event of even a short-term failure or loss. Normal = Assets / information can lead to moderate business-critical losses or damage in the event of a failure or loss over a longer period of time"	Information owner (division, department management or process owner)	 Encryption in transit		TLS1.2, TLS1.3, SSL, HTTPS etc.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Encryption for data storage		AES256, AES128 etc.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 "Are security standards adhered to during software development? If yes, which standards/best practices are adhered to (e.g. OWASP)?"		The provider adhere to the OWASP standard	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 "Is there a ""Security Whitepaper"" in which all security mechanisms and the (security) architecture are listed or described (e.g. MFA, SSO, ...)? If so, can the whitepaper be made available?"""		Security Whitepaper exists and can be viewed after signing an NDA.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 "Do information security certificates (e.g. ISO 27001) exist? If so, can security certificates be provided?"""		The provider is ISO 27001 certified.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 "Are regular penetration tests and/or internal/external security audits carried out? If so, can the results or the management summary be made available to us?"		"Yes, penetration tests are carried out every six months. The results can be provided after signing an NDA."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Contractual situation		There is currently no contract with the provider	HARTING GBU	 Start of contract			HARTING GBU	 End of contract			HARTING GBU	 "Security Directive ""Secure Network Connection"": https://wiki.harting.intra/node/12452"		Acknowledged	HARTING GBU	 				 "What are the tasks of an application owner? - If other users of the software have problems, the application owner is the first point of contact. This means that the application owner can either help with problems themselves or establish contact with the provider. - If other departments potentially want to use the software, the application owner is available to answer questions. - The application owner is responsible for ensuring that the system descriptions are always kept up to date. - The application owner is responsible for ensuring that all framework conditions specified in a release are actually implemented. - In addition, the application owner must ensure that the user administration is kept clean if a separate user administration exists."				On-Prem-Kataster Application: <insert application name here>		Examples	Who should work on the point?	 "System requirements of the server / appliance: - virtual systems under VMWare are a prerequisite"		"Virtual server in VMWare 64GB RAM 8 Core CPU etc."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Are databases required, if so, which ones		MySQL, SQL, etc.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Is a multi-system landscape planned (TST, DEV, PRD)?		PRD and TST only	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Which operating system is used		"Windows Server 2019, Linux Ubuntu, Linux Debian etc. Please specify the exact version."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Does the provider offer a maintenance contract? (obligatory)		Yes	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Does the system/application need to be monitored?		Yes (Monitoring is used to check whether the software/server is still running correctly)	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 How should data be backed up?		A full backup must be created initially and then an incremental backup must be created daily	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 "Are special components required for the application? e.g. Java licence etc. If critical components such as Log4j are required, the application owner/provider is responsible for ensuring that the versions used are secure."		Version xxx of log4j is required for the service.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Is it necessary to install a client or should the application be provided via Citrix?		A client must be installed on the end device in order to establish a connection with the application.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Please note:			HARTING GBU	 http://home.harting.intra/central/its/Richtlinien_ITIS/Vorgaben%20der%20IT%20f%C3%BCr%20IT-Systeme%20mit%20externer%20Unterst%C3%BCtzung%20EN.pdf		Acknowledged	HARTING GBU	Cloud-Kataster Application: <insert application name here>		Examples	Who should work on the point?	 Data contained, data types (e.g. name, e-mail, IP addresses, log files)		First name, surname, e-mail address of the user, job description, IP address, date of birth, invoice content, contract content	HARTING GBU	 Physical location(s) of data storage and processing (country and city)		"AWS Europe Azure Europe Azure USA etc."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Service platform (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)		SaaS	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Supported web browsers		Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Firefox, etc.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Supported operating systems for any necessary client-side software		"Not relevant. Works via all common browsers Linux is required as the cloud solution only communicates with the cloud solution via a local client."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Hardware requirements for any necessary client-side software		"HARTING Standard Hardware is sufficient, Physical Server is needed etc."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Access only from the office or also mobile?		Both	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Can access to company devices be restricted (e.g. via IP address)?		Yes, access can be restricted to company devices via conditional access policies.	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Owner of the data (only for company data; not relevant for personal data protection)		"Who is the owner of the data stored in the cloud? If the content is provided by HARTING and processed in the cloud, the HARTING Technology Group or the GBU is the owner of the data."	HARTING GBU	 User authentication / access protection (MFA etc.)		SSO via SAML2.0	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 What information security management processes are in place?		"e.g. Information Security Incident Management Business Continuity Management / Emergency management Information security risk management Security Awareness Security Monitoring"	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Storage/processing of data protection-relevant (personal) information		First name, surname, job description, date of birth	HARTING GBU	 Background of the data outsourcing		The provider has converted its on-premise solution to a cloud solution.	HARTING GBU	 Data storage: How is client separation ensured?		"Provider XY is a multi-client system with logical client separation. We take the following steps to ensure logical client separation: - We implement robust authentication and authorisation mechanisms - We secure our APIs: By filtering requests based on tenant IDs, we ensure that users can only access data that is assigned to their tenant. - We carry out regular tests and checks: Code reviews and external security tests help us to identify and fix potential vulnerabilities in tenant separation."	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 Storage duration (how the hoster handles storage and deletion)		30 days after termination of the contract	HARTING GBU with help from the provider	 				 				 				 				AI-Kataster Application: <insert application name here>		Who should work on the point? Positive and negative (ethical) effects (long-term) of the AI system:		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Responsibility for AI decisions - Who decides how the AI generates/selects the output? 		Provider Consequences in the event of damage / faulty output of the AI system, effects on HARTING in the event of faulty output of the AI system: 		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Type of AI system (e.g. generative AI, LLM, machine learning, deep learning)		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Training set data: What information was used for learning? Is the AI system self-learning?		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Input data: What is the input to the AI? (e.g. internal or external sources)		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Output data: What output does the AI produce? (e.g. texts, images, summaries, videos)		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Description of the general capabilities of the AI system:		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Hosting of the AI system (e.g. private instance or public instance):		HARTING GBU with help from the provider "Provider data re-use (dual use): Can the provider continue to use the data entered? Contractually or individually? How is the data further used (type and scope)? HARTING internally or externally?"		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Contractual agreements (e.g. on licences) must be attached:		HARTING GBU with help from the provider Risk classification according to the AI Regulation		HARTING GBU with help from the provider How is it ensured that no third-party rights are affected by the use of the output?		HARTING GBU with help from the providerClassification Classification:									 unrestricted									 restricted									 confidential									 strictly confidential									 Classification guide:									 Confidentiality category	Authorised use	Damage size1	Description of damage						 Unrestricted	Information that can be viewed by all internal and external persons without restriction.	none	The publication of the information does not imply any violation of regulations or requirements of the company of any kind.						 			There is no financial or reputational damage in the event of disclosure or misuse.						 Restricted	The information can be viewed by all employees of the HARTING Group. The need-to-know principle should be taken into account for access and viewing.	low to moderate	The financial damage caused by publication or misuse of the information is minor to moderate.						 			Only minor or only internal damage to reputation or trust is to be expected.						 			Incidents are only noticed externally in individual cases and considered insignificant						 			Violations of laws and regulations with minor/moderate consequences.						 Confidential	The information can only be viewed by a selected group of employees. The need-to-know principle must be taken into account for access and viewing.	serious	Access by unauthorised persons causes considerable or serious financial losses, but does not threaten the existence of the company.						 	In case of doubt, disclosure within the companies of the HARTING Technology Group is not permitted.		Incidents are clearly recognised externally / Image and trust in the company are impaired / Loss of image and trust must be compensated for at great expense.						 			Violation of laws and regulations with considerable consequences.						 Strictly confidential	The information can only be viewed by company management and explicitly authorised or designated employees.	catastrophic	The financial damage caused by access by unauthorised persons can be catastrophic or even threaten the existence of the entire group of companies.						 			Incidents are clearly recognised by the general public and/or discussed in the media. The image, trust and reliability of the entire group of companies are severely impaired and are fundamentally called into question.						 			Violations of laws and regulations with consequences for business operations. Liability claims can be very high or even ruinous.						 "1 Orientation ""Extent of loss in terms of turnover (U)"": low to moderate: 0 < U <= 5%; serious: 5% < U =< 10%; catastrophic: > 10% (The classes of extent of loss are based on the definition of Corporate Risk Management)." If so, can the white paper be made available?"""	0				 "Do information security certificates exist (e.g. ISO 27001)? If so, can security certificates be made available?"	0				 """Are regular penetration tests and/or internal/external internal/external security audits carried out? If yes, can the results or the management management summary be made available to us?"""	0				 Data storage: How is client separation ensured?	0				 "Storage duration (how the hoster handles storage and  deletion/description of the termination process)"	0				 					 Conclusion: 					 					 					Risikobewertung Eintrittswahrscheinlichkeit							Bewertung:	Anmerkung / Begründung		Schadensklassen							Bewertung:	Anmerkung / Begründung: 																		 Wahrscheinlichkeit	Beschreibung									Schadensklasse	Beschreibung							 impossible (0)	Das Ereignis kann nicht eintreten. Das System schließt den Eintritt des Ereignisses aus.									No Impact (0)	Ein Schaden ist nicht feststellbar. Weder Unternehmenswerte noch Geschäftsprozesse werden beeinträchtigt.							 very rarely (1)	Das Ereignis kann nur eintreten, wenn sehr spezielle Rahmenbedingungen gegeben sind, die in der Praxis eigentlich nicht vorkommen.									small (1)	Geschäftsprozesse werden nicht wesentlich gestört, Unternehmenswerte gehen nur in einem geringen Umfang verloren. Eine Wiederholung des Schadens muss nicht vermieden werden.							 rarely (2)	Das Ereignis kann eintreten, wenn spezielle Bedingungen gegeben sind. Diese können durchaus in der Praxis vorkommen.									moderate (2)	"Zentrale Geschäftsprozesse werden gestört aber nicht zum Erliegen gebracht, Unternehmenswerte gehen nicht in einem wesentlichen Umfang verloren. Der Schaden ist behebbar, ohne das wesentliche Mittel des Unternehmens eingesetzt werden müssen und bevor er sich ausweiten kann. Eine Wiederholung des Schadens soll vermieden werden."							 possible (4)	Der Eintritt des Ereignisses wird erwartet. Die Bedingungen für den Eintritt des Ereignisses sind häufig oder sogar permanent gegeben.									critical (4)	"Der Schaden stört zentrale Geschäftsprozesse empfindlich oder bringt diese zum Erliegen, es gehen wesentliche Unternehmenswerte verloren oder es werden Menschen verletzt. Der Schaden kann mit den Mitteln des Unternehmens behoben werden, bevor er den Bestand des Unternehmens unmöglich macht. Eine Wiederholung des Schadens muss vermieden werden."						x	z.B. Pozenziell sensible Zeichnungen können offengelegt werden. often (8)	"Das Ereignis wird nach menschlichem Ermessen eintreten. Das System entwickelt sich zwangsläufig auf den Eintritt des Ereignisses zu."									catastrophic (8)	"Der Schaden bringt zentrale Geschäftsprozesse zum Erliegen, zentrale Unternehmenswerte werden zerstört oder es kommen Menschen ums Leben. Der Schaden kann mit den Mitteln des Unternehmens nicht wieder behoben werden." Faktor und einem aktiven Monitoring der Logins, das bei Anomalien eine Benachrichtigung an die Admins sendet und unmittelbar eine Reaktion erfolgt.	personenbezogene Authentifizierung mit Passwörtern oder maschinenbezogene Authentifizierung und einem zusätzlichen 2. Faktor	unsichere Authentifizierung (MAC-Adressen, gemeinschaftlich genutzte Passwörter etc.), Schwache Passwörter	keine Authentifizierung				Folgen des Missbrauchs personenbezogener Daten für die gesellschaftliche Stellung oder wirtschaftliche Verhältnisse der Betroffenen	-	Daten sind nicht personenbezogen	keine	geringe	erheblich	ruinös		 Programme die die Verwundbarkeit ausnutzen	-	nicht existent, da nicht erstellbar	nicht existent	existent, jedoch öffentlich nicht verfügbar	öffentlich verfügbar, nur mit Fachwissen verwendbar	öffentlich verfügbar, ohne Fachwissen verwendbar				Konventionalstrafen infolge von Vertragsverletzung	-	keine	geringe	spürbare	hoch	ruinös		 Schadsoftware, die die Verwundbarkeit ausnutzt	-	nicht existent, da nicht erstellbar	nicht existent	existent, jedoch öffentlich nicht verfügbar	existent, arbeitet auf Basis unselbständiger Fortpflanzung (Virus)	existent, arbeitet auf Basis selbständige Fortpflanzung (Wurm)				Ausfallzeiten in der Produktion	-	keine	geringe	spürbare	erheblich	nicht zu tolerieren		 Weiterentwicklung der Eintrittswahrscheinlichkeit	-	wird sich sicher in absehbarer Zeit nicht erhöhen	wird sich wahrscheinlich in absehbarer Zeit nicht erhöhen	wird sich wahrscheinlich in den nächsten 12 Monaten nicht erhöhen	-	-				Finanzielle Folgen	-	keine	geringe	tolerabel	beachtlich	ruinös		 										interner Ansehens- und Vertrauensverlust für Unternehmen oder Führungsebene	-	nicht gegeben	nicht gegeben	gering	nachhaltig	grundlegend; Zusammenarbeit nicht mehr möglich		 										öffentlicher Ansehens- und Vertrauensverlust für Unternehmen oder Führungsebene	-	nicht gegeben	nicht gegeben	kleiner Teil der Kunden wird informiert	erheblicher Teil der Kunden wird durch Dritte (Presse) informiert	große Teile der Kunden und Interessenten wird durch Dritte (Presse) informiert		 										Gefahr für Geist, Leib und Leben von Personen.	-	absolut auszuschließen	extrem unwahrscheinlich	scheint nicht möglich	kann nicht ausgeschlossen werden	gegeben		 																		 																		 																		 Definition der Risikoklassen							Bewertung:	Anmerkung 										 																		 Risikoklasse	Bedeutung für das Unternehmen und Vorgehensweise																	 Bewertung fehlt	-																	 "No risk (0)"	Es besteht kein Risiko.																	 "Low (1-2)"	Es ist ein Risiko vorhanden, jedoch kann das Risiko vom Unternehmen generell akzeptiert werden. Maßnahmen können aber müssen nicht ergriffen werden. Falls Maßnahmen umgesetzt werden ist für sie nur ein geringer Aufwand in Kauf zu nehmen. Sie können z.B. so lange aufgeschoben werden, bis sie in Wartungs- oder Fortentwicklungsmaßnahmen der betroffenen Infrastruktur eingebettet werden können.																	 "Medium (4-8)"	"Generell kann das Risiko vom Unternehmen akzeptiert werden, die Entscheidung muss jedoch für jedes Risiko individuell getroffen werden. Wenn Schutzmaßnahmen umgesetzt werden, sollten sie maximal einen moderaten Mehraufwand im Unternehmen verursachen. "						x											 "High (12-16)"	Das Risiko kann generell nicht mehr akzeptiert werden - nur in Ausnahmefällen ist die Akzeptanz des Risikos möglich. Es sollten Schutzmaßnahmen mit hoher Priorität definiert, umgesetzt und evaluiert werden. Auch wenn die Umsetzung der Maßnahmen einen erheblichen Mehraufwand erfordert und nach sich zieht, darf sie nicht ausbleiben oder länger als zwingend notwendig aufgeschoben werden.																	 "Very High (>16)"	Das Risiko darf nicht akzeptiert werden. Im Interesse des Unternehmens und der Verantwortlichen müssen geeigneten Maßnahmen umgehend definiert, umgesetzt und evaluiert werden. Es ist Gefahr im Verzug. Es muss von den Verantwortlichen auch ein hoher Mehraufwand für die Implementierung und den Betrieb der Maßnahmen in Kauf genommen werden und kann/darf nicht als Begründung für eine Verzögerung oder Unterlassung dienen.																	 																		 																		 "Risikomatrix (""Standard"")"																		 																		 Eintritts-wahrscheinlichkeit	often (8)	"No risk (0)"	"Medium (4-8)"	"High (12-16)"	"Very High (>16)"	"Very High (>16)"												 	possible (4)	"No risk (0)"	"Medium (4-8)"	"Medium (4-8)"	"High (12-16)"	"Very High (>16)"												 	rarely (2)	"No risk (0)"	"Low (1-2)"	"Medium (4-8)"	"Medium (4-8)"	"High (12-16)"												 	very rarely (1)	"No risk (0)"	"Low (1-2)"	"Low (1-2)"	"Medium (4-8)"	"Medium (4-8)"												 	impossible (0)	"No risk (0)"	"No risk (0)"	"No risk (0)"	"No risk (0)"	"No risk (0)"												 		No Impact (0)	small (1)	moderate (2)	critical (4)	catastrophic (8)												 		Schadenshöhe im Eintrittsfall
